project.name: morsebrowser-react
project.description: React/React Native migration of the Long Island CW Club's Morse code practice trainer: a TypeScript monorepo with a shared Morse engine (@morsebrowser/core), a Vite + React + Bootstrap web app, and an Expo/React Native iOS app featuring native background audio for lock-screen practice. Note: scoped to exclude ~1100 bulk content/data records (word lists, preset JSON, binary assets) and focus on architecture.

nodes.name: morseScheduler.ts
nodes.summary: Schedules Morse code playback by pre-scheduling oscillator tones on the AudioContext clock with envelope shaping, avoiding setTimeout for timing accuracy.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses AudioContext render-quantum snapping and setValueCurveAtTime envelopes to achieve glitch-free, sample-accurate Morse timing.

nodes.name: timingEngine.ts
nodes.summary: Computes Morse timing parameters (unit durations, Farnsworth spacing, tone frequency) and builds the element schedule from text, including Koch lesson ordering.

nodes.name: wavExport.ts
nodes.summary: Renders Morse text to a downloadable WAV file via an OfflineAudioContext, including duration estimation and PCM WAV encoding.

nodes.name: morseMap.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the Morse and prosign character maps and provides bidirectional text/Morse conversion plus tokenization helpers.

nodes.name: lessonLoader.ts
nodes.summary: Loads and queries the lesson word-list catalog and fetches word/lesson files, resolving Koch lessons and random word-list configs.

nodes.name: mockContext
nodes.summary: Builds a mock AudioContext that captures scheduled frequencies for asserting scheduler behavior in tests.

nodes.name: buildEnvelopeCurve
nodes.summary: Generates an amplitude envelope curve with attack/release ramps to prevent audible clicks on Morse tone edges.

nodes.name: scheduleText
nodes.summary: Pre-schedules all oscillator tones for a Morse text on the AudioContext clock, applying Farnsworth timing and envelopes.

nodes.name: scheduleChar
nodes.summary: Convenience wrapper that schedules a single character via scheduleText.

nodes.name: scheduleTestTone
nodes.summary: Schedules a single test tone of a given duration on the AudioContext for tone preview and calibration.

nodes.name: toneFrequency
nodes.summary: Returns the oscillator frequency for the given timing config and tone type.

nodes.name: calculateFarnsworthTiming
nodes.summary: Computes Farnsworth character and word spacing durations from the WPM/effective-WPM config.

nodes.name: buildSchedule
nodes.summary: Converts Morse text into a timed element schedule (tones and gaps) using tokenization and Farnsworth timing.

nodes.name: kochCharsForLesson
nodes.summary: Returns the set of Koch-method characters introduced through a given lesson number.

nodes.name: estimateScheduleDuration
nodes.summary: Estimates the total playback duration of a Morse schedule to size the offline render buffer.

nodes.name: encodeWav
nodes.summary: Encodes a rendered AudioBuffer into a 16-bit PCM WAV byte buffer with a proper RIFF header.

nodes.name: renderMorseWav
nodes.summary: Renders Morse text to a WAV blob via an OfflineAudioContext using the scheduler and encoder.

nodes.name: tokenizeText
nodes.summary: Tokenizes input text into Morse-mappable units, optionally recognizing multi-character prosigns.

nodes.name: textToMorse
nodes.summary: Converts plain text into a Morse code string using the character/prosign map.

nodes.name: morseToText
nodes.summary: Decodes a Morse code string back into plain text using a reversed character map.

nodes.name: charToMorse
nodes.summary: Returns the Morse pattern for a single character or prosign.

nodes.name: getDisplays
nodes.summary: Filters the catalog to the displayable word-list options for a given target/class/letter-group selection.

nodes.name: loadWordList
nodes.summary: Fetches a word-list file by name and parses it into a trimmed array of words.

nodes.name: loadLessonFile
nodes.summary: Fetches a lesson file, parsing JSON or plain-text formats based on the file extension.

nodes.name: isRandomWordListConfig
nodes.summary: Type guard determining whether a value is a random word-list configuration object.

nodes.name: StartupHooks.tsx
nodes.summary: Headless component that runs startup-time side effects by invoking the useStartupUrlParams hook and rendering nothing.

nodes.name: StartupHooks
nodes.summary: Renderless component that triggers URL-param-driven startup configuration via useStartupUrlParams.

nodes.name: useStartupUrlParams.ts
nodes.summary: Hook that reads URL query parameters on first mount to seed app settings (voice, buffer length, noise) and manages a screen wake lock for the playback session.

nodes.name: useStartupUrlParams
nodes.summary: Applies startup URL parameters to MorseApp settings once and activates/deactivates a screen wake lock based on playback state.

nodes.name: lessonDeepLink.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for lesson deep-link resolution, verifying case-insensitive matching of class, group, and display URL parameters.

nodes.name: lessonDeepLink.ts
nodes.summary: Resolves lesson deep-link URL parameters (class, group, display) into a concrete lesson selection using case-insensitive lookups against catalog options.

nodes.name: resolveLessonDeepLink
nodes.summary: Reads class/group/display URL params and matches them against the lesson catalog to produce a resolved deep-link target plus the consumed params.

nodes.name: hasLessonDeepLinkParams
nodes.summary: Returns whether any lesson deep-link URL parameters are present in the current location.

nodes.name: screenWakeLock.ts
nodes.summary: Wraps the browser Screen Wake Lock API in a class that re-acquires the lock on visibility changes to keep the screen awake during playback.

nodes.name: ScreenWakeLock
nodes.summary: Manages acquisition and release of a navigator wake lock, reactivating it when the document becomes visible again.

nodes.name: urlParams.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for URL parameter helpers covering getUrlParam, hasUrlParam, and dev-build detection.

nodes.name: urlParams.ts
nodes.summary: Small helpers for reading window URL query parameters and detecting whether the app is running in a dev build.

nodes.name: isDevBuild
nodes.summary: Determines whether the current build is a development build based on URL hostname and query flags.

nodes.name: useMorsePlayer.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that owns the Web Audio context and exposes Morse playback primitives (playMorse, stopMorse, test tone) backed by the core scheduler, plus background-noise synchronization.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses refs to persist a single AudioContext and a NoisePlayer instance across renders; schedules Morse on the AudioContext clock rather than setTimeout.

nodes.name: useMorsePlayer
nodes.summary: Hook that lazily creates the AudioContext, wires the NoisePlayer, and returns memoized callbacks for scheduling Morse text, test tones, and stopping all audio.

nodes.name: noisePlayer.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the NoisePlayer class, verifying noise buffer generation and start/stop/sync behavior against a mocked AudioContext.

nodes.name: noisePlayer.ts
nodes.summary: Generates looping background noise (white/pink/brown) via Web Audio and exposes a NoisePlayer class to start, stop, sync, and set volume of the noise source.
nodes.languageNotes: Synthesizes noise into an AudioBuffer and plays it as a looping BufferSource with a gain node for volume control.

nodes.name: fillNoiseBuffer
nodes.summary: Fills a Float32Array with samples for the requested noise color (white, pink, or brown) using randomized DSP algorithms.

nodes.name: createLoopingNoiseSource
nodes.summary: Creates a looping AudioBufferSourceNode populated with synthesized noise of the given type for a given AudioContext.

nodes.name: NoisePlayer
nodes.summary: Stateful controller for a single looping noise source, supporting start, stop, volume control, and sync to current settings.

nodes.name: vite-presets-plugin.ts
nodes.summary: Vite dev/build plugin that serves preset JSON files from a sibling directory during development and copies them into the dist output at build time.

nodes.name: servePresetFile
nodes.summary: Resolves and streams a requested preset JSON file back over the dev server HTTP response, returning 404 when missing.

nodes.name: presetsDevPlugin
nodes.summary: Factory returning the Vite plugin object wiring preset middleware into the dev server and copying presets to the build output.

nodes.name: vite-wordfiles-plugin.ts
nodes.summary: Vite dev/build plugin that serves Morse wordfile data from a configurable directory in development and copies the files into dist during build.

nodes.name: serveWordfile
nodes.summary: Resolves and streams a requested wordfile over the dev server response, returning 404 if the file is not found.

nodes.name: wordfilesDevPlugin
nodes.summary: Factory returning the Vite plugin object that registers wordfile middleware and copies wordfiles to the build output.

nodes.name: vite.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vite configuration for the web app, registering the React plugin alongside the custom presets and wordfiles dev plugins.

nodes.name: settingsManager.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests for the settings manager covering cookie-backed load/save/reset using a mocked document.cookie store.

nodes.name: cookieStorage.ts
nodes.summary: Browser cookie read/write utilities with the KO-compatible cookie key map, used to persist user settings.

nodes.name: settingsManager.ts
nodes.summary: Loads, saves, and resets user settings to cookies, mapping between KO volume scaling and normalized values.

nodes.name: mockDocumentCookie
nodes.summary: Installs a mock document.cookie getter/setter backed by an in-memory store for deterministic settings tests.

nodes.name: getCookie
nodes.summary: Reads and URL-decodes a named cookie value, returning null when absent or outside a browser environment.

nodes.name: setCookie
nodes.summary: Writes a URL-encoded cookie with an expiry computed from a day count.

nodes.name: getAllCookies
nodes.summary: Parses document.cookie into a key/value record of all decoded cookies.

nodes.name: parseIntCookie
nodes.summary: Reads a cookie as an integer, returning a fallback when missing or non-numeric.

nodes.name: loadSettings
nodes.summary: Builds the settings object from persisted cookies, applying defaults and KO-to-normalized volume conversion.

nodes.name: saveSettings
nodes.summary: Persists the current settings to cookies, converting normalized volume back to the KO scale.

nodes.name: resetSettings
nodes.summary: Clears all known settings cookies to restore default state.

nodes.name: deploy.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that builds the web bundle and deploys the SPA to Cloudflare Pages and the RSS proxy to a Cloudflare Worker on pushes to master.
nodes.languageNotes: Single deploy job with steps: checkout, setup-node, npm ci, build web bundle, deploy RSS Worker, ensure Pages project, deploy Pages site. Uses a concurrency group to cancel in-progress runs and injects Cloudflare credentials via repository secrets.

layers.name: Shared Core Engine
layers.description: Framework-agnostic TypeScript Morse engine and shared types: morse map, timing, audio scheduler, lessons, presets, and cookie-backed settings consumed by both apps.

layers.name: Web Application
layers.description: Vite + React + Bootstrap web app — components, context, hooks, and utilities pixel-matched to the knockout.js fork.

layers.name: Mobile Application
layers.description: Expo / React Native iOS app — screens, components, context, hooks, and the native audio-session bridge for background/lock-screen practice.

layers.name: Mobile Native Platform
layers.description: Generated Android (Gradle/Kotlin) and iOS (Xcode/CocoaPods/Swift) native project scaffolding for the Expo app.

layers.name: Infrastructure & CI/CD
layers.description: Deployment pipeline and the Cloudflare RSS-proxy worker supporting the apps.

layers.name: Build & Tooling
layers.description: Monorepo build configuration and developer tooling: Turborepo, TypeScript configs, Vite plugins, package manifests, and the visual-diff harness.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project documentation: repo map, content-authoring guides, mobile status, and archived migration notes.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start here. The README frames the project: a TypeScript monorepo migrating the Long Island CW Club Morse trainer from knockout.js to React, with a shared engine, a web app, and a native iOS app that can play Morse through the lock screen.

tour.title: The Shared Core Engine
tour.description: All Morse logic lives in @morsebrowser/core, framework-agnostic so web and mobile share one source of truth. This barrel re-exports the engine, audio, lessons, and settings modules; the types package defines the shared contracts.

tour.title: The Morse Map
tour.description: The character-to-dits-and-dahs translation table — the foundation every other engine module builds on when turning text into Morse symbols.

tour.title: Timing Engine
tour.description: Computes precise dit/dah/gap durations from WPM, Farnsworth, and Koch settings. Accurate timing is a hard requirement — this module produces the schedule the audio layer plays.

tour.title: The Audio Scheduler
tour.description: The heart of the app: it pre-schedules every tone on the AudioContext clock with zero setTimeout, so Morse stays sample-accurate even under load. Understand this to understand why the migration exists.

tour.title: Lessons & Settings
tour.description: lessonLoader assembles Koch lessons and word lists; settingsManager persists user preferences (WPM, frequency, etc.) via cookies. Together they feed the engine its inputs.

tour.title: Web App Bootstrap
tour.description: How the web app boots: main.tsx mounts React, App.tsx assembles the Bootstrap UI pixel-matched to the original fork.

tour.title: Web Playback Flow
tour.description: State and playback wiring on web: MorseAppContext holds app state, useMorsePlayback drives the engine scheduler, and PlaybackControls is the user-facing transport.

tour.title: Mobile App & Native Audio
tour.description: The Expo/React Native iOS app. _layout.tsx is the expo-router entry; audioSession.ts configures the native audio session (configureAudioSession) that enables true background / lock-screen playback the browser cannot do.

tour.title: Mobile Playback Flow
tour.description: The mobile counterpart to step 8: MorsePlaybackContext and useMorsePlayback reuse the same core scheduler, with playbackOrchestration coordinating loops and seeks.

tour.title: Supporting Infrastructure
tour.description: A small Cloudflare Worker proxies RSS feeds for the apps, and the deploy workflow ships the web build. The leanest edge of the system.
